channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: El nuevo retrato familiar tiene cuatro patas y cola
item.description: Los retratos de mascotas por encargo existen desde hace siglos, pero ahora están llegando a un público más amplio.
item.dc:creator: Dorie Chevlen

media:content.media:credit: Christie Hemm Klok para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Quienes estén interesados en comisionar un retrato pueden comprarlos de artistas de formación clásica o a empresas de diseño digital.

item.title: Picture Prompt | April Fool’s Pranks
item.description: What are the best jokes or pranks you’ve seen or experienced — live or on the internet?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Scrolling through social media requires slightly more skepticism on April 1.

item.title: A ‘Traffic Jam’ of Trials Poses Headache for Mangione’s Lawyers
item.description: Luigi Mangione’s lawyers could be in and out of court in Manhattan through the end of the year. A judge could decide as early as Wednesday on whether to delay his federal case.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser and Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: Ahmed Gaber for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lawyers for Luigi Mangione have argued that his two trials, one in federal court and the other in state, should be delayed so he can prepare for the cases.

item.title: Who Murdered Minerliz? How a Diner Meet-up Cracked the Case.
item.description: In 1999, a 13-year-old girl was found dead in the Bronx. Then the trail went cold for 20 years, until an ingenious ploy by detectives produced a vital clue to her killer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Wilson

media:content.media:credit: Malcolm Jackson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Minerliz Soriano (in a photograph held by her best friend in middle school) was 13 when she was found murdered.

item.title: The Three Big Questions Delaying New York’s Budget
item.description: Legislators are weighing tax increases on the wealthy and changes to laws meant to protect immigrants and the environment as the state budget deadline passes.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Adrianna Newell for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: New York legislators passed a one-week extension to the April 1 deadline for approval of the state budget.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 1, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Stephen Colbert Blames ‘The Bachelorette’ Cancellation for $4 Gas
item.description: The “Late Show” host joked that “Trump attacked Iran” after ABC scrapped the reality show’s latest season.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Qué significa ‘gratuitous’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 61 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: gratuitous
item.description: This word has appeared in 61 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Qué pasó con el marido de Kristi Noem (y lo que piensan sus vecinos)
item.description: Antes y después de que The Daily Mail publicara las fotos, la sensación predominante entre los vecinos de la pareja fue la siguiente: era inevitable sentir lástima por Bryon Noem.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh

media:content.media:credit: Ben Brewer para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Castlewood no queda lejos de la granja familiar de los Noem en Dakota del Sur.

item.title: Stocks in Asia Rally and Oil Prices Drop
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. military would wrap up its campaign in Iran in two or three weeks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Joyce Zhou/Reuters

item.title: ¿Con qué frecuencia debo cambiar mis zapatillas de correr?
item.description: Correr con zapatillas desgastadas podría aumentar tu riesgo de lesiones. Estas son algunas señales de que necesitas un par nuevo.
item.dc:creator: Allessandra DiCorato

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas para The New York Times

item.title: Hanami en Japón: ¿cuándo florecen los cerezos?
item.description: Los expertos utilizan la inteligencia artificial para analizar datos, junto con miles de fotos publicadas por el público, para predecir la aparición de las preciadas flores, que son una atracción multimillonaria.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández, Kiuko Notoya, Hisako Ueno and Kentaro Takahashi

media:content.media:description: Visitantes reunidos bajo los cerezos en flor en el parque Ueno de Tokio la semana pasada.

item.title: Padma Lakshmi: The Decision That Would Create a Permanent American Underclass
item.description: President Trump wants to take away a fundamental American promise.
item.dc:creator: Padma Lakshmi

media:content.media:credit: Chuck Ramirez

item.title: Where Million-Dollar Coastal Homes Glitter Near Metal Shacks
item.description: A shortage of affordable housing in the coastal city in South Africa has forced many people to live far outside the city center, while tourists occupy prime real estate.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Joao Silva

media:content.media:credit: Joao Silva/The New York Times

item.title: The Sailors Stranded in the Persian Gulf
item.description: Thousands of civilian sailors have been stranded for more than a month in waters surrounded by a conflict zone because of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aie Balagtas See
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Altaf Qadri/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Almost 20,000 seafarers are stranded in the Persian Gulf, according to the International Maritime Organization, as Iran has effectively blocked the exit through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: In UK Prisons, Drones Fly In Contraband ‘As if by Uber Eats’
item.description: Decades-old prison buildings were designed to be secure from the ground but not the air. Experts say that makes a lucrative smuggling trade hard to tackle.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden

item.title: Cape Town’s Housing Problem
item.description: The rise of tourist rentals in Cape Town is driving up housing costs, deepening the inequality in the South African city that’s a legacy of apartheid. Our reporter John Eligon explores a multihour commute taken by workers who are priced out of the city.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon, Joao Silva, Christina Thornell, Jon Miller, Leila Medina and June Kim

item.title: Quote of the Day: Trump Facing Hard Decision As War Rages
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, April 1, 2026.

item.title: YouTube’s C.E.O. on the Rise of Video and the Decline of Reading
item.description: On “The Interview,” Neal Mohan, YouTube’s C.E.O., talks about the platform’s role in an age of post-literacy and his belief that video serves as a vital “visual library” for a new generation of learners.

item.title: Appeals Court Stops V.O.A. Journalists From Quickly Returning
item.description: A panel of three judges halted the lower court order to reinstate all full-time Voice of America reporters and support staff who had been put on paid leave.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: United States Agency for Global Media

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voice of America office building in Washington.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 1, 2026
item.description: Jess Shulman and Amie Walker’s crossword? It’s kind of an inside joke.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Bomb Shelters and a Drone-Proof Roof: Trump Says Ballroom Is a Matter of Security
item.description: President Trump spoke about his ballroom’s security features as he argued against a judge’s orders to stop construction.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A federal judge on Tuesday said that work on the White House ballroom had to stop until the project received a go-ahead from Congress.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Will Be Out of Iran Within Two to Three Weeks
item.description: The White House said the president would address the nation about Iran on Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump told reporters on Tuesday that he had attained his goal of dealing with Iran’s nuclear program and that gasoline prices in the United States would be coming down.

item.title: Trump Says He Halted Nuclear Threat From Iran, Despite Evidence to the Contrary
item.description: For the second time in recent days, President Trump declared that one of the key objectives of the war had been accomplished.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump has narrowed his Iran war objectives in recent days.

item.title: Trump Administration Acted Illegally With Homeless Grants Program, Judge Rules
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island said that the administration had wrongly demanded that groups seeking grants comply with its agenda on other issues, including immigration.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeParle

media:content.media:credit: Jim Wilson/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: San Francisco officials clear homeless encampments from city streets after a Supreme Court ruling.

item.title: Cesar Chavez fue la voz de gente como yo. Ahora, estamos de luto
item.description: El cofundador de la Unión de Campesinos era celebrado como un ejemplo de los derechos civiles. Pero una investigación del Times descubrió numerosas pruebas de que abusaba de las mujeres.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley

media:content.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un mural dedicado a Cesar Chavez, entre otros, fue cubierto en San Juan, Texas, tras las acusaciones de que el cofundador de la Unión de Campesinos sedujo y abusó sexualmente de jóvenes que trabajaban en el movimiento.

item.title: Editors’ Note: April 1, 2026
item.description: The following Editors’ Note and corrections appeared in print on Wednesday, April 1, 2026.

item.title: Israel Strikes Tehran Pharmaceutical Plant, Claiming Military Link
item.description: Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said on Tuesday that Israel was “openly and unashamedly bombing pharmaceutical companies.”
item.dc:creator: Parin Behrooz and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Damaged apartments in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: South Dakotans React to Daily Mail Article on Bryon Noem, Kristi Noem’s Husband
item.description: In the tiny town of Castlewood, S.D., where everyone knows the Noems, the prevailing sense was that people can’t help but feel bad for Bryon Noem after a tabloid photo leak.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh

media:content.media:credit: Ben Brewer for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Castlewood is not far from the Noem family farm in South Dakota.

item.title: Trump Signs Order Seeking Federal Control of Mail Voting as He Promotes False Claims
item.description: Election experts and Democratic officials called the order legally invalid, and Arizona and Oregon pledged to immediately challenge it in court.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times

item.title: Tugboat Captain Is Charged in Miami Beach Crash That Killed 3 Children
item.description: Six people, including a camp counselor, were thrown into the water on July 28 when their sailboat was struck by a barge that was being pushed by a tugboat, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz and Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Joe Raedle/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An aerial view of Biscayne Bay surrounding Hibiscus Island in Miami Beach, where a barge struck a sailboat in July, killing three children.

item.title: Judge Dismisses Lawsuit That Challenged Ban on Endorsements by Churches
item.description: Conservatives had expected a victory in the case after the I.R.S. agreed to a settlement that allowed churches to voice support for candidates.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold and Elizabeth Dias

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A federal judge in Texas on Tuesday dismissed a lawsuit seeking to overturn the 70-year-old ban on political activity by churches.

item.title: Justice Dept. Struggles to Respond to Trump’s Suit Against IRS
item.description: Officials at the department and the White House are in the middle of a messy and complicated debate over how to respond to President Trump’s lawsuit demanding $10 billion from the I.R.S.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump filed a lawsuit demanding at least $10 billion from the I.R.S.

item.title: Arkansas 2026: Live Primary Runoff Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Arkansas primary runoff elections.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Massachusetts State House 5th Essex District Special 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Massachusetts 5th Essex District special election.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Arkansas Secretary of State Primary Runoff 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Arkansas primary runoff election.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: South Africa Sees Maritime Traffic Surge Amid Iran War
item.description: Global shipping companies looking for safer routes are turning to the Cape of Good Hope, a much longer, more expensive journey.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Gianluigi Guercia/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Cargo vessels moored at the harbor in Cape Town in South Africa.

item.title: Drivers Say Rising Gas Prices Are ‘Out of Control’
item.description: Gasoline prices in the U.S. passed $4 a gallon, on average, as the war in the Middle East affects global energy markets. Drivers said that the rising costs were affecting their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Charly Triballeau/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Forgoing Oversight of Iran War, G.O.P. Calls Hegseth to Testify on Budget
item.description: After resisting calls for public hearings for weeks, House Republicans have called the secretary of defense to testify at a budget hearing in late April for the first time since the attacks on Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at a news conference at the Pentagon in Arlington, Va., on Tuesday.

item.title: Slava Tsukerman, Who Directed the Cult Classic ‘Liquid Sky,’ Dies at 86
item.description: A Russian-born director, he created a film about New Wave models and killer aliens in 1980s New York, helping to reshape independent filmmaking in America.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, took questions from reporters for the first time in nearly two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: BTS Is Back, But at What Cost?
item.description: BTS is back. Roll up to the drive-through with our critic Jon Caramanica as he dissects “Normal,” one of the most inventive songs from the group’s new album “Arirang,” and explains how it taps into the stylings of Rihanna and Mk.gee.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: U.N. Peacekeepers in Lebanon Were Killed by Roadside Bomb
item.description: Members of the United Nations Security Council condemned deadly attacks on U.N. peacekeepers in south Lebanon and called for de-escalation between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi

media:content.media:credit: Kawnat Haju/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon earlier this month.

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects a Ban on ‘Conversion Therapy’ for Minors
item.description: Also, a judge halts construction on the White House ballroom. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Melvin Edwards, Sculptor Who Wove Black History Into Art, Dies at 88
item.description: Lynch Fragments, a series of abstract steel sculptures he created starting in 1963, evoked the long, devastating history of violence against Black Americans.
item.dc:creator: Will Heinrich

media:content.media:credit: Chester Higgins Jr./The New York Times

item.title: Backdoor Funding of Homeland Security Agency Could Weaken Congress Anew
item.description: An emerging Republican plan to skirt a Democratic filibuster and fund an entire department without congressional appropriations would be the latest example of surrendering power to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Border Patrol agents in Minneapolis in early January. Using the complex budget process known as reconciliation to fund the entire Homeland Security Department, as Republicans are suggesting, would be a significant departure from traditional congressional practice.

item.title: At the National Archives, the Declaration Gets More Company
item.description: The Emancipation Proclamation and the 19th Amendment have been added to the Archives’s rotunda, the first permanent changes there in nearly 75 years.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Schuessler

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Visitors looked at the original copy of the 19th Amendment this week. The document was recently added to the permanent display in the rotunda at the National Archives in Washington.

item.title: Trump’s Presidential Library Video Draws Mixed Reactions From Miami Residents
item.description: The president’s foundation released a video showing a gleaming tower by Biscayne Bay emblazoned with his name. It would dominate the skyline.
item.dc:creator: Audra D. S. Burch, David C. Adams and Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Kirby Lee/Imagn Images, via Reuters Connect
media:content.media:description: President Trump plans to use the prime property with bay views donated to him by the state of Florida to maximum effect.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Military Campaign to Wind Down and Plans National Address
item.description: “We will be leaving very soon,” the president told reporters in the Oval Office. A few hours later, the White House said Mr. Trump would provide “an important update on Iran” on Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
